title: Sweetspot Build Manifest
description: This schema defines a build manifest that combines project identity, source inputs, dependencies, verification, security, release evidence, and provenance. Build-system authors, release operators, and auditors need it when they generate or validate a distributable build record. Read it before publishing an artifact or comparing build evidence across environments. Remember that its source, checks, and produced artifacts must refer to the same run.

x-sma-contract.change_policy: Optional additive fields preserve v1 compatibility; required, renamed, or narrowed fields require a new versioned $id and schema_version.
x-sma-contract.rollback: Consumers retain the v1 decoder during the compatibility window; rollback drops only additive optional fields and never rewrites immutable or historical evidence.
x-sma-contract.lifecycle: Created or regenerated by the listed writer, validated at its consumer boundary, and retained according to docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md.
x-sma-contract.retention: See docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md for the artifact-specific retention rule.
x-sma-contract.performance: Static file contract; database query plans, indexes, and full-scan budgets are not applicable. Validation performance is covered by schema-case selftests.

schema_version.description: Schema version that this document conforms to.

domain.description: Ordered domain values for the build.

runtimes.description: Ordered runtimes values for the build.

trust_tier.description: Selected trust tier; allowed values are `experimental`, `reviewed`, `verified`, `canonical`.

project.description: Project identifier associated with the source.

commit.description: Git commit identifier associated with the recorded work.

paths.description: Repository-relative paths associated with the source.

derived_from_bricks.description: Ordered derived from bricks values for the source.

supporting_artifacts.description: Ordered supporting artifacts values for the source.

reviewers.description: Ordered reviewers values for the owner.

brick_refs.description: Ordered brick refs values for the composition.

flows.description: Ordered flows values for the composition.

optional_bricks.description: Ordered optional bricks values for the composition.

alternatives.description: Ordered alternatives values for the composition.

shared_contracts.description: Ordered shared contracts values for the composition.

data_classes.description: Ordered data classes values for the classification.

ssa_v2.description: Structured value recorded for ssa version 2.

entrypoints.description: Ordered entrypoints values for the interface.

ui_surfaces.description: Ordered ui surfaces values for the interface.

events.description: Ordered events values for the interface.

api_endpoints.description: Ordered API endpoints values for the interface.

commands.description: Ordered commands values for the interface.

required.description: Ordered required values for the environment.

optional.description: Ordered optional values for the environment.

forbidden.description: Ordered forbidden values for the environment.

inputs.description: Ordered inputs values for the data.

outputs.description: Ordered outputs values for the data.

stores.description: Ordered stores values for the data.

tables.description: Ordered tables values for the data.

required.description: Whether required applies to the auth.

modes.description: Ordered modes values for the auth.

roles.description: Ordered roles values for the auth.

rls.description: Fields that describe the row-level security.

required.description: Whether required applies to the row-level security.

tables.description: Ordered tables values for the rl.

matrix_path.description: Recorded matrix path for the row-level security.

negative_tests.description: Tests that prove forbidden access or cross-boundary behavior fails.

outbound_hosts.description: Ordered outbound hosts values for the network.

webhooks.description: Ordered webhooks values for the network.

inbound_endpoints.description: Ordered inbound endpoints values for the network.

latency_budget_ms.description: Latency budget ms as a whole number for the performance.

queue_allowed.description: Whether queue allowed applies to the performance.

concurrency_hint.description: Concurrency hint as a whole number for the performance.

fixture_targets.description: Ordered fixture targets values for the verification.

smoke_commands.description: Ordered smoke commands values for the verification.

integration_targets.description: Ordered integration targets values for the verification.

evidence.description: Ordered evidence values for the verification.

last_verified_at.description: Time recorded for last verified at, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

greenfield_support.description: Whether greenfield support applies to the clone.

matching_architecture_support.description: Whether matching architecture support applies to the clone.

required_ports.description: Ordered required ports values for the clone.

file_map.description: Ordered file map values for the clone.

install_steps.description: Ordered install steps values for the clone.

post_clone_checks.description: Ordered post clone checks values for the clone.

rollback_steps.description: Ordered rollback steps values for the clone.

known_traps.description: Ordered known traps values for the clone.

target_docs.description: Ordered target docs values for the clone.

compatibility_policy.description: Selected compatibility policy; allowed values are `manifested_ports_only`, `same_runtime_family`, `manual_adapter_review`, `project_specific`.

migration_hooks.description: Ordered migration hooks values for the upgrade.

supersedes.description: Ordered supersedes values for the upgrade.

replacement_policy.description: Selected replacement policy; allowed values are `automatic_if_contract_compatible`, `manual_review_if_contract_changes`, `manual_only`.

rollback_supported.description: Whether rollback supported applies to the upgrade.

breaking_change_signals.description: Ordered breaking change signals values for the upgrade.

publishable.description: Whether publishable applies to the publishing.

redaction_profile.description: Selected redaction profile; allowed values are `internal-default`, `community-default`, `public-hardened`, `custom`.

openness.description: Declared redistribution openness. The license-lattice gate blocks any value more open than the MEET of the build's component bricks.

lattice.description: Computed lower bound from the component bricks (written by sma-license-gate). Advisory record of what the composition actually permits.

effective_openness.description: Selected effective openness; allowed values are `closed`, `source-available`, `open`.

effective_visibility.description: Selected effective visibility; allowed values are `private`, `internal`, `community`, `public`.

most_restrictive_brick.description: Most restrictive brick for the lattice.

computed_at.description: Time recorded for computed at, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

exposed_docs.description: Ordered exposed docs values for the publishing.

excluded_assets.description: Ordered excluded assets values for the publishing.

estimated_prompt_token_savings.description: Estimated prompt token savings as a whole number for the economic.

estimated_clone_time_minutes.description: Estimated clone time minutes as a whole number for the economic.

estimated_update_time_minutes.description: Estimated update time minutes as a whole number for the economic.

maintenance_score.description: Maintenance score as a whole number for the economic.

provenance.description: Evidence describing where the content came from and who changed it.

created_by.description: Structured value recorded for created by.

touched_by.description: Ordered touched by values for the provenance.

reviewed_by.description: Ordered reviewed by values for the provenance.

source_chain.description: Ordered source chain values for the provenance.

provenance_seal.description: Tamper-evident hash chain over provenance events anchored to the content fingerprint. Generated by tools/sma-provenance-ledger.mjs and verified by tools/sma-provenance-verify.mjs.

chain_length.description: Chain length as a whole number for the provenance seal.

events_digest.description: Events digest for the provenance seal.

key_id.description: Key identifier for the provenance seal.

status.description: Current status of the gate score.

score.description: Score as a whole number for the gate score.

notes.description: Additional notes about the gate score.

evidence.description: Ordered evidence values for the gate score.

name.description: Human-readable name of the environment var.

required_in.description: Ordered required in values for the environment var.

forbidden_in.description: Ordered forbidden in values for the environment var.

command.description: Command used to produce or verify this evidence.

status.description: Current status of the verification event.

timestamp.description: Time when the event occurred, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

output_path.description: Path to the stored command output or evidence artifact.

notes.description: Additional notes about the verification event.

actor_kind.description: Category of actor that performed the action.

actor_id.description: Identifier of the human, agent, automation, or tool that performed the action.

model.description: Model identifier recorded for an AI-driven actor.

session_id.description: Identifier of the agent session that produced this value.

task_id.description: Identifier of the task associated with this value.

commit.description: Git commit identifier associated with the recorded work.

files_touched.description: Repository-relative files changed by the recorded work.

timestamp.description: Time when the event occurred, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

brick_id.description: Stable identifier of the brick associated with this record.

required.description: Whether required applies to the build brick ref.

order.description: Order as a whole number for the build brick ref.

project.description: Project identifier associated with the build brick ref.

path.description: Repository-relative path to the build brick ref.

notes.description: Additional notes about the build brick ref.

id.description: Stable identifier of the build flow.

name.description: Human-readable name of the build flow.

steps.description: Ordered steps values for the build flow.

notes.description: Additional notes about the build flow.

id.description: Stable identifier of the build step.

name.description: Human-readable name of the build step.

order.description: Order as a whole number for the build step.

brick_refs.description: Ordered brick refs values for the build step.

inputs.description: Ordered inputs values for the build step.

outputs.description: Ordered outputs values for the build step.

notes.description: Additional notes about the build step.

brick_ids.description: Ordered brick ids values for the build alternative.

notes.description: Additional notes about the build alternative.

source_path.description: Repository-relative source path supplied by the reusable brick.

target_path.description: Repository-relative destination path for the placed file or directory.

notes.description: Additional notes about the file map entry.

project.description: Project identifier associated with the build source event.

artifact_id.description: Artifact identifier for the build source event.

artifact_type.description: Selected artifact type; allowed values are `brick`, `build`, `release`, `fixture`.

commit.description: Git commit identifier associated with the recorded work.

path.description: Repository-relative path to the build source event.

timestamp.description: Time when the event occurred, in ISO 8601 date-time format.
